workItem.title: Treat capacity waiting as queue state, not an execution retry

## Context

With max_concurrent_agents set to one, game-api#80 reached a clean published repair HEAD but repeatedly received no available orchestrator slots. Each capacity miss incremented its retry attempt and exponential due time, allowing newly polled issues to take the next slot and starving the older recovery.

## Expected outcome

Eligible work that lacks capacity remains in an ordered ready queue without consuming attempts or backoff; recovered and post-approval work receives deterministic priority over new work.

## Scope

- Separate capacity_waiting from execution_failure
- Do not increment Attempt or retry counters when no slot exists
- Preserve FIFO and explicit priority across polling cycles
- Prioritize merging, rework and recovered running issues over new ready issues
- Expose queue position and reason in the dashboard
- Add saturation and restart scheduling tests

## Out of scope

- Increase concurrency beyond configured safety limits
- Bypass repository dependencies
- Treat genuine agent or provider failures as capacity waits

## Acceptance criteria

- No-slot events create no Attempt and consume no retry budget
- An older recovered issue receives the next available slot before newly discovered ready work
- agent:merging work cannot starve behind agent:ready work
- Queue order survives process restart
- Complete Symphony scheduler tests pass
